en_title: Ex-Forza Horizon Director Traces a Line From the Failure of 'Well-Meaning' Game Pass to the Current Xbox Layoffs
cn_title: 前《极限竞速：地平线》总监：XBOX Game Pass未达预期导致裁员
subtitle: 订阅规模撑不起巨额收购

paragraphs.en: As the fallout from the Xbox reset continues, one former developer of the Forza Horizon series has said what we’re seeing with layoffs, studio exits, and game cancelations is the result of the failure of Game Pass to hit the subscriber numbers Microsoft required to justify its enormous spending on content.
paragraphs.cn: 随着XBOX重置的余波持续发酵，一名前《极限竞速：地平线》系列开发者表示，如今的裁员、工作室出走和游戏取消，源于Game Pass订阅人数未达到微软为巨额内容投入提供合理性的目标。

paragraphs.en: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has already cut 1,600 staff from Xbox, and plans for another 1,600 to go over the course of the current financial year. Four studios are confirmed to be leaving Microsoft ownership, with another on its way. Sharma has called it the most “significant” restructure in Xbox history, insisting Microsoft’s gaming business “is not healthy.”
paragraphs.cn: XBOX首席执行官Asha Sharma已裁减1600名员工，并计划在本财年再裁1600人。已有4家工作室确认脱离微软，另有一家也将离开。Sharma称这是XBOX史上最「重大」的重组，并直言微软游戏业务「并不健康」。

paragraphs.en: Speaking on MinnMax , Mike Brown, who was creative director on the hugely successful Forza Horizon 5 before leaving developer Playground to found his own studio, Maverick Games, which is set to release an open world racer of his own called Clutch, said that Xbox Game Pass had failed to hit enough subscribers to justify Microsoft’s huge spending on content acquisition. This includes the eye-watering $69 billion Microsoft splashed out on Call of Duty developer Activision Blizzard.
paragraphs.cn: 曾担任热销作品《极限竞速：地平线5》创意总监的Mike Brown在MinnMax节目中表示，XBOX Game Pass未能吸引足够多订阅用户，无法证明微软巨额内容收购支出的合理性，其中包括以69 billion，即690亿美元(约合人民币4671亿元)收购《使命召唤》开发商动视暴雪。Brown离开Playground后创办Maverick Games，正准备推出自己的开放世界竞速游戏《Clutch》。

paragraphs.en: “I think it's really sad and I think it's really, really unfortunate,” Brown began. “They've honestly invested a fortune in the concept of Game Pass as a service, which led to loads of teams being acquired, loads of games being funded, loads of people getting jobs, all with a vision of getting regular games landing in this service for players. I think the reality is not enough people have subscribed to it at the prices that they needed to pay in order to make that business viable.
paragraphs.cn: Brown开场说：「我觉得这真的很悲哀，也真的非常不幸。他们确实为Game Pass服务概念投入了巨资，由此收购许多团队、资助许多游戏、创造许多岗位，愿景是定期为玩家把游戏带到这项服务中。但现实是，没有足够多的人愿意按维持这门生意所需的价格订阅。」

paragraphs.en: “And, that’s actually just really sad. Because we're now going to see studios getting shut down or spun off, and loads of people are going to lose their jobs, and that is honestly just a result of a well-meaning, player-focused concept of Xbox Game Pass not quite landing.”
paragraphs.cn: 「这实在令人难过，因为我们现在会看到工作室关闭或被剥离，大量员工失去工作。老实说，这就是XBOX Game Pass这个本意良好、以玩家为中心的概念没能真正落地的结果。」

paragraphs.en: Brown went on to defend Microsoft and Game Pass, praising the concept of the subscription service and the games that would otherwise not have been made were it not to have existed.
paragraphs.cn: Brown随后为微软和Game Pass辩护，称赞订阅服务的理念，以及那些若没有该服务便可能根本不会诞生的游戏。

paragraphs.en: “It’s just a real shame,” he continued. “And as much as people point fingers and call different people within Xbox the villain of the piece, the concept of Game Pass was good. Like, it's a good idea. Let's have a subscription, make it affordable, try and get games to more people. We'll have a really diverse selection of games on there. We'll get a load of studios involved. We'll make some games that probably couldn't exist otherwise. And if more people had subscribed, that makes enough money and it works, and all of those studios go on and those games get made again and those teams will make other games. Unfortunately, it didn’t.
paragraphs.cn: 他继续说：「这真的很可惜。人们尽管会指责XBOX内部不同的人，把他们当成反派，但Game Pass的概念本身是好的。它是个好主意：推出价格亲民的订阅，让更多人接触游戏；提供非常多样的游戏阵容，让许多工作室参与，制作一些原本可能无法存在的作品。如果有更多人订阅，它就能赚到足够的钱并运转下去，所有工作室都能继续，游戏可以再做，团队也能开发其他作品。遗憾的是，事情没有如此发展。」

paragraphs.en: “I don’t know the number it needed to hit was, but it never reached the number that would make a $70 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard make financial sense. It’s really unfortunate. We don’t know what the future is just yet, but it is a reshaping of the business around that, after the first concept didn’t quite land.”
paragraphs.cn: 「我不知道它究竟需要达到多少用户，但它从未达到足以让近70 billion，即700亿美元(约合人民币4739亿元)收购动视暴雪在财务上说得通的规模。这真的很不幸。我们尚不知道未来会怎样，但在最初概念没能落地后，公司正在围绕这个现实重塑业务。」

paragraphs.en: Brown’s comments here actually echo those of Sharma herself. When she announced the layoffs, Sharma admitted that Game Pass had failed to grow as Microsoft had expected.
paragraphs.cn: Brown的说法其实与Sharma本人相呼应。Sharma宣布裁员时承认，Game Pass未能像微软预期那样增长。

paragraphs.en: “Our business today is not healthy,” Sharma said. “We are operating at margins that are 3-10x lower than comparable platform and publishing businesses. We entered Gen 9 with a smaller install base and a higher cost structure. To grow, we bet on Game Pass, multi-platform, and a broader portfolio of content. While those businesses have created meaningful value, they did not grow at the pace we expected. As that happened, our core business weakened, and we added more teams, more investment, and more time, hoping for a better outcome. And now the industry is facing the most severe hardware crisis in its history. We must reset Xbox.”
paragraphs.cn: Sharma说：「我们今天的业务并不健康。我们的利润率比同类平台和发行商业务低3至10倍。进入第九世代时，我们的装机基础更小，成本结构却更高。为了增长，我们押注Game Pass、多平台和更广泛的内容组合。这些业务确实创造了有意义的价值，却没有按预期速度增长。与此同时，核心业务走弱，我们又投入更多团队、资金和时间，希望得到更好结果。如今行业还面临史上最严重的硬件危机。我们必须重置XBOX。」

paragraphs.en: The Wall Street Journal said that Microsoft had expected Game Pass subscriptions to hit around 77 million this year, but it currently has only about 30 million. As revealed during the FTC vs Microsoft trial of 2023, Microsoft had hoped for 100 million subscribers by 2030, which seems very unlikely at this stage.
paragraphs.cn: 《华尔街日报》称，微软原本预计Game Pass今年订阅人数将达到约77 million，即7700万，但目前只有约30 million，即3000万。2023年美国联邦贸易委员会诉微软案披露，微软曾希望到2030年达到100 million，即1亿订阅用户；现阶段看来，这一目标极难实现。

paragraphs.en: The exact number of subscribers Microsoft had in early 2026 is unclear, but we do know Game Pass had at least 34 million members as of February 2024 . By July 2025, Xbox Game Pass revenue had reportedly reached nearly $5 billion for the first time.
paragraphs.cn: 微软在2026年初的确切订阅人数尚不清楚，但已知截至2024年2月，Game Pass至少拥有34 million，即3400万名会员。据报到2025年7月，XBOX Game Pass收入首次接近50亿美元(约合人民币338亿元)。

paragraphs.en: One of the first decisions Sharma made after replacing former Xbox boss Phil Spencer was to cut the price of Game Pass and pull Call of Duty out as a day one title. Sharma recently told Bloomberg that Xbox had "been able to reset Game Pass after an eight-month decline." "It’s now returned to growth and expanding retention," she added, "and most importantly, we're starting to get back to being closer to our players and our community."
paragraphs.cn: Sharma接替前XBOX负责人菲尔·斯宾塞后，最早作出的决定之一便是降低Game Pass价格，并取消《使命召唤》首发加入服务。她最近告诉彭博社，XBOX已「在连续8个月下滑后成功重置Game Pass」。她补充说：「它现在已恢复增长，用户留存也在扩大；最重要的是，我们正重新拉近与玩家和社区的距离。」

paragraphs.en: Still, the future of Game Pass is unclear. Will Microsoft pull its games out of the subscription as day one launches? Will Bethesda's The Elder Scrolls 6 launch day one on Game Pass? Will the next mainline Halo game? Could we see Game Pass become more like Sony’s PlayStation Plus, which does not launch first-party games day one?
paragraphs.cn: 尽管如此，Game Pass的未来仍不明朗。微软会不会取消自家游戏首发加入订阅？贝塞斯达的《上古卷轴6》会首发进入Game Pass吗？下一部《光环》正传呢？Game Pass会不会变得更像索尼PlayStation Plus，不再让第一方游戏首发加入？

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, Microsoft is doubling down on its biggest franchises, which for now means more Fallout more quickly .
paragraphs.cn: 与此同时，微软正加倍投入旗下最大型系列；眼下，这意味着以更快速度推出更多《辐射》内容。

opus_summary: 前《极限竞速：地平线5》创意总监Mike Brown认为，Game Pass本意良好，却未达到支撑微软巨额投入所需的订阅规模，最终导致裁员、工作室剥离和项目取消。微软称服务现已恢复增长。
